name: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.
window: Q1-2026 print → intra-quarter · Apr 30 → Jul 16, 2026 (2Q prelim Jul 7; full print Jul 30)

events.text: Q1-2026 — a record quarter in AI memory. Revenue KRW 134tn (+43% q/q); OP KRW 57tn (+185% q/q); operating margin 43% (from 21%); EPS KRW 7,123. DS KRW 81.7tn (+225% y/y), memory KRW 74.8tn (+292% y/y). DRAM ASP +low-90s% q/q, NAND ASP +high-80s% q/q. HBM4 in mass production since Feb 2026, "fully booked," HBM sales >3x y/y in 2026; HBM4E samples in Q2. Foundry Taylor TX move-in on Apr 23, mass production 2027. 2026 capex with a "substantial increase."

FinTwit memory books underweight Samsung vs SK Hynix on the HBM/yield advantage; SK Hynix ~5.3x vs Samsung ~5.9x fwd P/E. Hyperscalers diversifying away from TSMC engage Samsung Foundry (AMD/Google/Tesla) for availability despite lagging yields.
JPM flagged a union strike with 7-12% downside to 2026 OP (briefing).

Three engines firing from 2H26 — memory + foundry + robotics. Q2-26 OP KRW 100tn vs his estimate of KRW 84tn; "the cheapest it will get" at 4.7x '27 P/E. DRAM shortage "with no way out for the next few years."

High-NA EUV done well is "an advantage" via design simplification and ~75% fewer process steps vs low-NA double patterning; the half-field/stitching constraint is "not as serious as we thought"; DRAM "eminently suited" for high-NA — a structural opening for Samsung Foundry/memory to leapfrog TSMC (a slow high-NA adopter) if it executes.

JPM's call that Samsung would close the gap with Hynix post-strike "didn't work" — Hynix kept outperforming on passive/single-stock ETF flows + an ADR catalyst. Samsung catalysts: a real start to HBM4 volume production, a larger AMD chiplet/foundry order at US Foundry Day (late-June/early-July), and a shareholder-return update (~October). Near-term EPS drag: the union-settlement provision (~12% of profit) hits Q2, ~2pts above JPM's prior 10%.
5-6x earnings disbelieves the durability of the cycle; 9x would be "conservative" (briefing).

HBM commoditizes once 2nd/3rd vendors qualify, but Samsung's cost structure is actually better than Micron's — relatively advantaged in a snap-back, but still at the cyclical top. New capacity (Samsung/SK/Micron) arriving ~CY27-28 could break pricing.
Server DRAM C3Q26 +30-40% q/q — DRAM ASP trajectory (a test of Jefferies' KRW 100tn OP estimate for Q2-26).
DRAM 1d (7th generation) mass production, earlier in 1H27 (briefing). The Korean tape rose +4.8% overnight on memory pass-through from Apple.

DRAM shortage "with no way out for the next few years"; foundry optionality is incremental upside as hyperscalers diversify away from TSMC — TSMC advanced-node capacity pre-booked into 2028+ pushes AMD, Google (Axion ~2028 / TPU), Tesla to engage Samsung Foundry.
Samsung Foundry signed a strategic deal to make 'Claros' power-management chips for AI data centers — AI-DC foundry traction.
Memory super-cycle tape — a melt-up in conventional DRAM ASP + the HBM5 roadmap (2028-29); Samsung framed as the HBM4 catch-up story vs SK Hynix.

Samsung exits 2026 with the largest DRAM wafer capacity at ~740K wafers/month (vs SK Hynix ~610K, Micron ~365K), adding only ~45K in 2027 (clean-room is the constraint until end-2028); P5L phase-1 only ready mid-2028, phase-2 announced with output ~2030. NAND→DRAM conversion drives global NAND cap -3% in 2026 and Samsung loses NAND share. A real possibility Samsung overtakes SK Hynix as #1 in HBM in 2027. Anthropic's HBM deal (2028) with Samsung implies an in-house ASIC.

HBM4 price still not finalized two weeks ago "despite Samsung already shipping," expected +90-100% vs last year's LTA. First disclosure that Samsung has a hybrid-bonding project and is "willing to offer it at a premium" (for taller stacks / HBM4E) — read bullishly by Besi holders. Samsung said that if wafers are short it would optimize the mix — pushing conventional DRAM at the expense of HBM (a TSV backend penalty), further limiting HBM capacity.

events.src: JPM · Joshua Meyers / MS · Kathleen Oh (Korea mega-plan)
events.text: Korea AI mega-plan revised up to ~US$3.1tn (Samsung + SK Group, KRW4,755tn through 2040 for semis/AI-DC/robotics) — supersedes the $880B/$1.3T framing within 48hrs. ~60-70% front-end wafer / 20-30% infra+cleanroom; implies doubling installed Korean DRAM capacity within 5yrs. Samsung's own KRW2,450tn plan extends geographically + in HBM (Cheonan/Onyang HBM fabs, KRW625tn Honam, Gwangju hub); memory capacity to double by 2031. Rothschild reads the headline as partly 'political repackaging.'

2Q26 preliminary OP W89.4tn beat the lowered Street (W75-84tn) after ~W15tn+ 1H26 labor provisions; sales ~W171tn; memory OPM ~80% DRAM / ~65% NAND; NAND ASP +>70% QoQ, DRAM below JPMe on mix; 4nm HBM4 base-die entered full-scale mass production. Yet the stock fell ~7% (6th KOSPI circuit breaker of 2026) — Samsung briefly overtook NVIDIA/Apple as the world's most profitable company but sold off, El-Erian: 'the market's hurdle for memory names has risen dramatically.' JPM OW, PT W480,000 (full detail Jul 30). BofA/Simon Woo: W400-500tn annual OP sustainable into 2027-28.

Positioning washout: Samsung Electronics -9.9% on the day; Citi estimates leveraged-ETF hedging forces ~$878m of SEC selling into the close, SEC breaking its ~W230k 1-Apr retail entry level (expects a bounce off the 100-DMA toward ~W270k). Goldman PSI: SEC foreign ownership 46.6% (2009 low), 13-day foreign KOSPI sell streak, Asia single-stock LETF complex ($22.5bn 2x Hynix/Samsung) violently unwinding — 'the most crowded trade globally.' Flow/positioning, not fundamentals.

Memory correction overdone: conventional DRAM ASP +50-60% QoQ; Korea's lower 2Q blended ASP (~low-mid 40% QoQ) is a 2Q-specific mix effect (stable HBM px + high 1Q base), NOT a 3Q trend — supportive of Samsung memory earnings into the Jul 30 print. Jefferies' Silk Road desk (Titu) upgraded Samsung to BUY the same day.

'CXMT: A Deeper Look Ahead of IPO' — China-DRAM-threat quantification: CXMT ~8% DRAM rev share / ~10% capacity ship share, 290k→350k wspm 2026 (target 800k), blended ASP ~24% discount to the Big-3, cost ~$0.27/Gb vs ~$0.20-0.21/Gb, ~93% of bits on 16nm, HBM 2-4yr behind (HBM3E ~2027). WF: role seen 'predominantly filling a supply void,' ex-China use limited on quality/US-control. Low-end creep, not a near-term HBM threat.

'Samsung's foundry division has agreed to manufacture Anthropic's custom AI chips' (industry source) — upgrades the prior 'in talks' (07-02) to a reported agreed 2nm win; the 07-15 report lists Anthropic + DeepX among newly secured 2nm customers alongside Google/Tesla, with TSMC's capacity-constrained overflow 'flowing to Samsung.' Taylor also completed Tesla's A15/AI5 tape-out (2nd US 2nm chip) and will fab the I/O die of Google's 2nm 'Icefish' TPU — real wins, but still under JPM/Gokul's ~5-6% 2nm-share cap (overflow, not leadership).

cells: Best memory sales ever; HBM3E + server SSD
Positioned as the recovery engine in 2H
HBM sales to triple y/y in 2026
HBM4 mass production Feb; "fully booked," >3x y/y
Utilization up; 2nm GAA ramp in Q4
Double-dgt y/y; Taylor TX move-in; 1.4nm on track
